# Standalone Social Visual Specs

First implementation targets blog/GitHub Pages, LinkedIn, Medium, Substack, and LinkedIn Article.

## Platform Dimensions

| Platform use | Dimensions | Format | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Blog companion infographic | 3200x2080 | PNG | 10 x 6.5 in at 320 DPI |
| Blog inline architecture diagram | 3200x1800 | PNG or Mermaid | Wide layout for readability |
| LinkedIn card | 1080x1350 | PNG | Portrait cards maximize feed presence |
| LinkedIn square carousel | 1080x1080 | PNG -> PDF | Use when existing carousel grammar requires square |
| LinkedIn multi-image exhibit | 1200x627 | PNG | Use for executive mode |
| Medium hero | 1400x800 | PNG | Use after hook |
| Medium inline | 1200x800 | PNG | Use at data-heavy moments |
| Substack Note hero | 1200x630 | PNG | One strong visual plus short excerpt |
| LinkedIn Article exhibit | 1200x627 | PNG | 2-3 inline exhibits |

## Copy Budgets

- LinkedIn card: 25-45 words.
- Comic panel caption: 8-16 words.
- Infographic body: 80-140 words total.
- Executive exhibit: conclusion title plus short subtitle; data labels do the work.
- Medium/Substack visuals: captions can add context, so the image should stay cleaner.

## Source Lines

Every metric-bearing image needs a visible source line. Format:

```text
Source: OpenAI Evals docs, 2026; SWE-bench, 2026
```
